name: scripts
context: Run package scripts, local binaries, and temporary CLIs in pnpm with run, exec, dlx, and recursive filters
info: This skill covers pnpm's execution surface rather than dependency mutation: `pnpm run` for package scripts, `pnpm exec` for binaries available to the project, and `pnpm dlx` for one-off tools fetched from the registry. It matters because recursive execution, `--parallel`, `--resume-from`, `--shell-mode`, `PNPM_PACKAGE_NAME`, and report summaries all change how an autonomous agent should run tasks in a workspace.
triggers: execute a local binary with pnpm
use pnpm dlx for a one off cli
resume a pnpm exec from one package
body:
# pnpm Script and Command Execution

pnpm has three distinct execution surfaces:

- `pnpm run <script>` for package.json scripts
- `pnpm exec <command>` for binaries already available to the project
- `pnpm dlx <package>` for temporary tools fetched on demand

```bash
pnpm run build
pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
pnpm -rc exec 'echo $PNPM_PACKAGE_NAME'
pnpm dlx create-vue my-app
```

Use recursive flags only when a task should fan out across a workspace. Otherwise prefer a single filtered package or current-directory execution.

sections.title: run vs exec vs dlx
sections.body: `run` knows about package scripts and their pre/post hooks. `exec` runs a command with the project's binary resolution and, in recursive mode, exposes `PNPM_PACKAGE_NAME`. `dlx` fetches a package from the registry without adding it to dependencies and hot-loads its default binary. The official `dlx` docs also support `--package` for extra helper packages and note that `pnpx` is an alias.

sections.title: Recursive execution
sections.body: With `-r`, pnpm executes across workspace projects. The `exec` docs say `--parallel` ignores concurrency and topological sorting, which is appropriate for long-running independent tasks but not for dependency-ordered builds. `--resume-from <package>` lets a large workspace restart from a named package after failure. Always pair recursion with [[pnpm/workspace#filters]] unless the whole workspace is intended.

sections.title: Shell mode and reporting
sections.body: Use `--shell-mode` or `-c` when the command needs pipes, env expansion, or shell syntax. On UNIX pnpm uses `/bin/sh`, and on Windows it uses `cmd.exe`. `--report-summary` is useful when a higher-level agent wants machine-readable run metadata instead of only streamed logs. For dependency freshness checks before execution, combine this node with `verifyDepsBeforeRun` from [[pnpm/config#workspace-settings]].

links.context: Workspace selectors and recursive semantics are shared across pnpm commands
Install dependencies before relying on local binaries or package scripts
Temporary tools fetched by dlx may also need build-script review

related.note: Recursive script execution depends on workspace selectors and package boundaries
Local binaries and scripts generally require an up-to-date install

scripts.name: Run Command
scripts.description: Dispatch pnpm run, exec, or dlx with recursive, filtered, and shell-aware options

CI.description: Affects some script behavior when tools branch on CI detection

PNPM_HOME.description: Optional pnpm home directory if the binary is not on the default PATH

target.description: Script name, command name, or package name

parallel.description: Ignore topological ordering for recursive runs

depends.reason: Most run and exec workflows need dependencies installed first
Recursive execution should be paired with explicit workspace selectors
